clinical_rationale: Regular blood glucose monitoring for Type 1 diabetes management per ADA 2025 standards; recommended at least 4 times daily (fasting, before meals, before bed)
Hemoglobin A1c monitoring for Type 1 diabetes glycemic control assessment; target <7% for most nonpregnant adults
Blood pressure monitoring for cardiovascular risk assessment in Type 1 diabetes per ADA 2025 guidelines
Blood pressure monitoring for cardiovascular risk assessment in Type 1 diabetes per ADA 2025 guidelines
Regular blood glucose monitoring for Type 2 diabetes management per ADA 2025 standards; frequency varies based on treatment regimen
Hemoglobin A1c monitoring for Type 2 diabetes glycemic control assessment; target <7% for most nonpregnant adults
Blood pressure monitoring for cardiovascular risk assessment in Type 2 diabetes per ADA 2025 guidelines
Blood pressure monitoring for cardiovascular risk assessment in Type 2 diabetes per ADA 2025 guidelines

Enhanced blood glucose monitoring for gestational diabetes per ADA 2025 guidelines; multiple daily measurements (fasting + postprandial); targets: fasting <95 mg/dL, 1h postprandial <140 mg/dL, 2h postprandial <120 mg/dL
Monthly A1c monitoring during pregnancy for gestational diabetes; target <6% in second and third trimesters if achievable without significant hypoglycemia
Blood pressure monitoring for preeclampsia risk assessment in gestational diabetes
Blood pressure monitoring for preeclampsia risk assessment in gestational diabetes
Enhanced systolic BP monitoring during pregnancy for preeclampsia screening per ACOG Practice Bulletin 222; alert threshold ≥140 mmHg, urgent threshold ≥160 mmHg
Enhanced diastolic BP monitoring during pregnancy for preeclampsia screening per ACOG Practice Bulletin 222; alert threshold ≥90 mmHg, urgent threshold ≥110 mmHg
Weight monitoring for appropriate gestational weight gain and fluid retention detection; alert for >2 lbs/week gain in 2nd/3rd trimester
Weekly systolic BP monitoring for gestational hypertension management per ACOG 2020; monitoring for progression to preeclampsia
Weekly diastolic BP monitoring for gestational hypertension management per ACOG 2020; monitoring for progression to preeclampsia
Intensive systolic BP monitoring for preeclampsia management per ACOG 2020; severe features require immediate treatment if ≥160 mmHg
Intensive diastolic BP monitoring for preeclampsia management per ACOG 2020; severe features require immediate treatment if ≥110 mmHg

Enhanced systolic BP monitoring for hypertension management per AHA/ACC 2025 guidelines; home BP monitoring recommended with 2 measurements, 1 min apart, twice daily for 3-7 days before clinic visits; target <130 mmHg
Enhanced diastolic BP monitoring for hypertension management per AHA/ACC 2025 guidelines; home BP monitoring recommended; target <80 mmHg
Daily weight monitoring for heart failure fluid management per 2022 ACC/AHA/HFSA guidelines; alert for 2-3 lbs gain in 1 day or 5+ lbs in 1 week
Enhanced systolic BP monitoring for heart failure management per 2022 ACC/AHA/HFSA guidelines; assess hemodynamic status and medication response
Enhanced diastolic BP monitoring for heart failure management per 2022 ACC/AHA/HFSA guidelines; assess hemodynamic status and medication response
Oxygen saturation monitoring for heart failure patients per 2022 ACC/AHA/HFSA guidelines; assess for hypoxemia and supplemental oxygen needs
Heart rate monitoring for heart failure medication titration and arrhythmia detection per 2022 ACC/AHA/HFSA guidelines
Enhanced respiratory rate monitoring for COPD management per GOLD 2025 report; >30 breaths/min indicates acute respiratory failure
Oxygen saturation monitoring for COPD exacerbation detection per GOLD 2025 report; <92% requires arterial blood gas assessment; target 88-92% if on supplemental oxygen during exacerbations
Enhanced systolic BP monitoring for CKD progression prevention per KDIGO 2021 guidelines; target <120 mmHg using standardized office measurement for most non-dialysis patients
Enhanced diastolic BP monitoring for CKD progression prevention per KDIGO 2021 guidelines; home BP monitoring recommended to complement office readings
